_comment: Spec #3 §5 + Spec #2 §12 AC-02 golden for NIST CFReDS Hacking Case.
This file enumerates the 14 canonical findings a correct Find Evil! run
against the SCHARDT.001 image should surface. Recall target: >= 10 of 14
(71%) per AC-02. Mapping of finding_id to artifact/offset/tool_call_id is
populated by the agent at run time; we only declare WHAT must be found.
These are derived from the NIST case documentation + SANS FOR508
walkthroughs. Truly novel findings emerge at run time via ACH agents.
The M2 chain-of-custody manifest signs the full list cryptographically.

findings.description: Dual-boot XP install with linked-list of recent searches for hacking tools
findings.artifact_hint: Registry hive USRCLASS / NTUSER.DAT search history

findings.description: USB device insertion history indicates external drive connected for staging
Recovered deleted email discussing the intrusion plan
Hacking tool artifacts in Program Files / downloaded applications
Prefetch evidence of hacking tool execution
Internet history indicating downloads of illicit content
Shellbag entries show navigation to removable media holding staged files
LNK (shortcut) files referencing removable media volume serial numbers
Recycle bin contains deleted staging artifacts
User account with suspicious naming / elevated privileges created
MRU list shows recently opened files consistent with intrusion planning
Event log entries indicating user logons consistent with claimed timeline
Thumbcache / thumbnail artifacts indicating viewed content
Named-pipe or service enumeration artifacts consistent with reconnaissance
findings.artifact_hint: Sysmon EID 17/18 (if present); service control manager events
